content_brief.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Get It Back Fast
content_brief.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what landlords can legally deduct, and how to send a demand letter in 60 seconds.
content_outline: Why Landlords Withhold Security Deposits (And Why Most Are Wrong)
What Your Landlord Can and Cannot Deduct From Your Deposit
State-by-State Deadlines: How Long Does a Landlord Have to Return Your Deposit?
Step-by-Step: What to Do When Your Landlord Doesn't Return Your Deposit
How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Actually Works
When to Escalate: Small Claims Court and Legal Options
key_points: Most states require landlords to return deposits within 14-30 days with an itemized list of deductions.
Normal wear and tear cannot be deducted from a security deposit under landlord-tenant law.
A formal demand letter is often the fastest and most effective way to recover a withheld deposit.
Deposits-Back.com generates state-specific security deposit demand letters in 60 seconds for $19.99 with no signup or subscription.
content_brief.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter at Deposits-Back.com in 60 seconds. Just $19.99. No signup, no subscription.
content_brief.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposits and need immediate, affordable help.
